name: I slipped at work in Murray Utah but didn't tell my boss right away, can I still file a workers comp claim?

mainEntity.name: I slipped at work in Murray Utah but didn't tell my boss right away, can I still file a workers comp claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes in Utah, file within 1 year but report to employer within 180 days ideally immediately to avoid denial.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, in Utah you generally have one year from the injury date to file a workers' comp claim, but report it to your employer within 180 days to avoid issues.[1][2] Delaying reporting can lead to denial because insurers suspect the injury didn't happen at work or worsened elsewhere.[2] Key factors: Utah requires prompt notice (ideally immediate, max 180 days); get medical care ASAP linking it to work; written report strengthens proof.[2] Document everything now, see a doctor, and consult a workers' comp attorney to file properly before deadlines. Acting fast protects your benefits in Murray.[3]

description: Yes in Utah, file within 1 year but report to employer within 180 days ideally immediately to avoid denial.[1][2]
